policy.relationship: Claude and Codex artifacts are peer implementations of shared agent behavior. Neither side is the canonical source; each side may be edited first.
policy.maintenance: When one side changes, the active agent must update the counterpart in the same session. Hooks remind immediately and block one-sided commits unless the changed artifact is synchronized or this manifest records an explicit artifact-specific status or exemption.

instructions.name: global-instructions
instructions.equivalence: Equivalent after tool-specific instruction normalization; Claude @context syntax and Codex absolute context/tool paths are compared semantically. The live ~/.codex/AGENTS.md symlink points at the tracked Codex global instruction file.

skills.name: move-session-log
skills.note: Claude and Codex move-session-log skills intentionally differ because each tool stores sessions in its own layout. The Claude version operates on ~/.claude/projects, ~/.claude/history.jsonl, and ~/.claude.json (a script-free Bash/Glob skill); the Codex version operates on ~/.codex/sessions, history.jsonl, session_index.jsonl, and state_5.sqlite via a bundled Python script. They must not be forced to byte-parity.

hooks.name: block-destructive-command.sh
hooks.note: paired; Claude ask decisions become deny-with-user-confirmation instructions; Codex side normalizes string-wrapped tool_input via lib-codex-hook-json.sh

hooks.name: block-elpaca-dotfiles-edit.sh
hooks.note: paired for apply_patch/Edit/Write path guards

hooks.name: block-elpaca-rebuild-uncommitted.sh
hooks.note: paired for Bash emacsclient guard; Codex side normalizes string-wrapped tool_input via lib-codex-hook-json.sh

hooks.name: block-github-guard-edit.sh
hooks.note: paired self-protection for the shared GitHub write allowlist, guard scripts, tracked codex/hooks.json, live ~/.codex/hooks.json, and live ~/.claude/settings.json; Claude handles Edit/Write/NotebookEdit file_path payloads, while Codex handles apply_patch/Edit/Write changed paths via lib-codex-paths.sh

hooks.name: block-github-write-command.sh
hooks.note: paired GitHub write gate for shell commands; blocks git push and mutating gh operations unless the target repo is in agents/github-write-allowlist.txt. Codex side normalizes string-wrapped tool_input via lib-codex-hook-json.sh; Claude side is delegated by pretooluse-bash.sh.

hooks.name: block-secret-leak.sh
hooks.note: paired; Codex version scans Bash and apply_patch payloads; Codex side normalizes string-wrapped tool_input via lib-codex-hook-json.sh

hooks.name: block-unguarded-ahrefs-api.sh
hooks.note: paired for Bash/exec_command raw Ahrefs API guards; allows the free subscription-info quota endpoint and the ahrefs-api-guard wrapper, blocks paid api.ahrefs.com shell calls that have not checked the live workspace reserve

hooks.name: block-sensitive-read.sh
hooks.note: paired; Codex side gates Bash/exec_command sensitive-path reads and includes a Read branch for future tool-name compatibility, with Codex deny decisions replacing Claude ask decisions

hooks.name: block-texi-edit.sh
hooks.note: paired for apply_patch/Edit/Write path guards

hooks.name: block-walk-list-access.sh
hooks.note: paired; Codex version scans Bash and apply_patch paths; Codex side normalizes string-wrapped tool_input via lib-codex-hook-json.sh. The Read|Grep|Glob|NotebookEdit branches in the case statement are unreachable on the Codex side (Codex does not dispatch those tool names) but are kept for structural parity with the Claude counterpart; documented in the script header.

hooks.name: block-long-pipeline-run-unchecked.sh
hooks.note: Claude PreToolUse Bash gate blocking long consensus-trader pipeline launches (setup_db.py --full/--target, refresh_data.sh) unless the tree is clean, checkpointing is on, and PREFLIGHT=ok is set. Project-scoped to the consensus-trader repo. No Codex counterpart: the pipeline is driven from Claude Code.

hooks.name: check-chrome-profile-pair.sh
hooks.note: Claude Chrome SessionStart no-op placeholder; not relevant to Codex.

hooks.name: elpaca-run.sh
hooks.note: Claude Emacs hook utility; Codex routes Emacs events through codex-hook-wrapper directly.

hooks.name: fire-and-forget.sh
hooks.note: Claude utility wrapping notify-emacs-* hooks so the CLI doesn't block on emacsclient. The Codex copy was never registered and was removed when the redundant Codex notify-emacs-hook.sh registrations were dropped in favor of codex-hook-wrapper.

hooks.name: guard-external-actions.sh
hooks.note: paired for MCP tool names; Ahrefs MCP allowlist intentionally covers only docs and subscription-info tools because paid read-only Ahrefs analytics calls consume shared workspace units

hooks.name: lib-codex-hook-json.sh
hooks.note: Codex hook payload normalizer for tool_input/tool_response object-or-string variants; Claude hooks read native Claude payloads directly.

hooks.name: lib-codex-paths.sh
hooks.note: Codex apply_patch path extraction helper; uses lib-codex-hook-json.sh for object-or-string payload normalization.

hooks.name: lib-repo-root.sh
hooks.note: Shared repo-root helper copied for hooks that need command-context git roots; Codex usage comment references lib-codex-hook-json.sh.

hooks.name: lib-staged-files.sh
hooks.note: Shared staged-file helper copied for commit gates; Codex usage comment references lib-codex-hook-json.sh.

hooks.name: load-elisp-after-edit.sh
hooks.note: paired for apply_patch/Edit/Write path lists; Codex PostToolUse output must include hookEventName and use additionalContext rather than Claude's message field

hooks.name: notify-emacs-notification.sh
hooks.note: Claude Notification event; Codex routes Notification-equivalent events through codex-hook-wrapper directly.

hooks.name: notify-emacs-session-end.sh
hooks.note: Claude Stop event wrapper; Codex routes Stop through codex-hook-wrapper directly.

hooks.name: pretooluse-bash.sh
hooks.note: Claude Bash PreToolUse dispatcher registered in ~/.claude/settings.json. It ports cheap Bash guards in-process and delegates heavier git/filesystem checks, including the GitHub write guard, to their standalone hook scripts. Its Bash command rewrite emits the full original tool_input with only command replaced so Claude's replacement-style updatedInput preserves timeout/run_in_background. Codex registers the corresponding shell hooks directly in codex/hooks.json instead of using this dispatcher and has no Bash command-rewrite counterpart.

hooks.name: regenerate-manual-after-edit.sh
hooks.note: paired for apply_patch/Edit/Write path lists; Codex PostToolUse output must include hookEventName and use additionalContext rather than Claude's message field

hooks.name: regenerate-coverage-map.sh
hooks.note: Claude PostToolUse Edit|Write hook that refreshes rubric-visualizer coverage maps after CR task grading/rubric.md or grading/coverage-map.json edits. Codex has no matching global PostToolUse hook for this Trajectory-specific Claude workflow.

hooks.name: register-coverage-hook.py
hooks.note: One-shot helper used to register regenerate-coverage-map.sh in ~/.claude/settings.json; kept beside the runtime hook for local maintenance. It is not a runtime hook and has no Codex counterpart.

hooks.name: redact-secrets.sh
hooks.note: Claude Bash output redaction filter used by wrap-bash-output.sh; Codex does not use this Bash command-rewrite hook path.

hooks.name: remind-ai-config-sync.sh
hooks.note: paired as Claude/Codex wrappers around bin/ai-config-sync

hooks.name: remind-claude-readme-update.sh
hooks.note: paired for Codex apply_patch/Edit/Write path lists; Codex PostToolUse output must include hookEventName and use additionalContext rather than Claude's plain-text reminder. Codex side was re-indented and the in-dotfiles-repo short-circuit was lifted out of the per-path loop (cosmetic; Claude side was already correctly structured because it processes a single file_path).

hooks.name: remind-plan-review.sh
hooks.note: paired PostToolUse reminder after Superpowers implementation plan files are edited, including repo docs/superpowers/plans paths and tool-specific temp/private plan directories; Claude emits plain stdout for Edit/Write, while Codex scans apply_patch/Edit/Write path lists and returns PostToolUse additionalContext JSON.

hooks.name: require-ai-config-sync.sh
hooks.note: paired as Claude/Codex wrappers around bin/ai-config-sync

hooks.name: require-codex-readme-update.sh
hooks.note: Codex README commit gate for Codex integration files; scoped to the dotfiles repo so unrelated repositories with codex/ or bin/ai-config-sync paths are not blocked; uses lib-codex-hook-json.sh for object-or-string payload normalization. The command-text fallback deliberately ignores .codex/ paths because project-local skill commits can stage .codex/skills before the hook can reliably resolve the target repo. The repo-root project AGENTS.md mirror is also excluded so routine Latest session bookkeeping does not require Codex README churn. Dotfiles maintenance skills are an intentional repo-root project-local exception and live under dotfiles/.claude/skills and dotfiles/.codex/skills.

hooks.name: require-doc-update.sh
hooks.note: paired for Bash git commit gate; Codex side normalizes string-wrapped tool_input via lib-codex-hook-json.sh

hooks.name: require-elisp-skill.sh
hooks.note: Depends on Claude Skill PostToolUse markers; Codex has no matching Skill hook event in this setup.

hooks.name: require-elisp-test-before-commit.sh
hooks.note: paired for Bash git commit gate; Codex side normalizes string-wrapped tool_input via lib-codex-hook-json.sh

hooks.name: require-elisp-verify-after-commit.sh
hooks.note: paired for Bash post-commit verification gate; Codex side normalizes string-wrapped tool_input via lib-codex-hook-json.sh

hooks.name: require-readme-update.sh
hooks.note: paired for Claude config README gate; Codex side normalizes string-wrapped tool_input via lib-codex-hook-json.sh

hooks.name: session-learning-capture.sh
hooks.note: Paired legacy Stop helper kept for tool parity, but it is not registered in live ~/.claude/settings.json or codex/hooks.json because Stop fires at turn end rather than true session exit. Session learning capture is prompted by the Emacs agent-before-exit path configured in emacs/config.org.

hooks.name: sync-agent-c-worktree.sh
hooks.note: Claude SessionStart hook for Trajectory agent-c worktrees: repairs ALL agent-c worktree key symlinks on every session start (the repair runs before the cwd/origin guards, so it works even when a session launches from the umbrella root ~/Trajectory and not just the one cwd worktree — this is the mechanical guarantee that a session never hits a missing ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and asks Pablo to run `ln -s`), restores upstream AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md before merging origin/main, then reapplies Pablo's local-only trimmed instruction overlay, prepending ~/Trajectory/AGENTS.md so Codex worktree sessions get the Trajectory parent context despite each agent-c worktree being its own Git root. It also injects Pablo's private, project-scoped (non-user-level) skills from ~/Trajectory/.claude/skills into the worktree's .claude/skills (globbed, not hardcoded, so new private skills are picked up automatically; symlinks are added to the worktree-local git exclude so they never appear in status or get committed), making them available in agent-c sessions without polluting unrelated Claude sessions. The bulk wrapper bin/sync-agent-c-worktrees calls this hook across task worktrees after one fetch by setting SYNC_AGENT_C_SKIP_FETCH=1, preserving the same per-worktree guards without redundant network fetches. Codex has no matching SessionStart hook in this setup; creation-time overlay is handled by the Emacs agent-trajectory-new-task command, and existing worktrees can be synced manually with the shared syncagentc shell function or the bulk wrapper. The hook also garbage-collects stale transient CR QA worktrees under ~/Trajectory/agent-c/.cr-tmp/ via bin/cr-worktree-gc.sh, removing only clean, fully-pushed throwaways and keeping anything with uncommitted or unpushed work; Codex has no SessionStart equivalent, so a leaked worktree is reclaimed at the next Claude session or by running cr-worktree-gc.sh manually. As of 2026-07-03 it also (a) idempotently registers the cr-issue-comment-freshness.sh PreToolUse guard into each worktree's untracked .claude/settings.local.json (merge, never clobber; the tracked public settings.json is never touched), and (b) prints a `cr-task-state --hook` banner — a deterministic map of which branch/run/rubric revision is live for the worktree's task, with divergence warnings — so a fresh session never resolves task state by trial-and-error (the 2026-07-03 wrong-lineage QA incident). Both additions are best-effort and non-fatal.

hooks.name: track-elisp-skill.sh
hooks.note: Depends on Claude Skill PostToolUse events; Codex has no matching Skill hook event in this setup.

hooks.name: track-elisp-test.sh
hooks.note: paired for Bash post-tool tracking; Codex side normalizes string-wrapped tool_input/tool_response via lib-codex-hook-json.sh

hooks.name: track-elisp-verify.sh
hooks.note: paired; Codex version accepts exitCode and exit_code payload keys and normalizes string-wrapped tool_input/tool_response via lib-codex-hook-json.sh

hooks.name: wrap-bash-output.sh
hooks.note: Claude PreToolUse Bash command wrapper that pipes output through redact-secrets.sh and preserves non-command Bash tool_input fields when emitting replacement-style updatedInput; Codex does not use this Bash command-rewrite hook path.

hooks.name: cr-skill-routing-reminder.sh
hooks.note: Claude PreToolUse Edit/Write reminder: fires when editing a CR prompt/rubric/GOLDEN, telling the agent authoring is owned by /article-to-rubric (QA by /qa-reasoning) and to invoke the skill rather than hand-author. Non-blocking additionalContext. CR-scoped; no Codex peer.

hooks.name: cr-card-body-gate.sh
hooks.note: Claude PreToolUse Bash gate: denies a GitHub issue create/edit command when its --body-file is a CR card attempt that fails cr_card_body.py check (missing biblio/author/abstract), forcing CR cards through the template tool. Scoped to CR-card bodies; no Codex peer.

hooks.name: cr-issue-comment-freshness.sh
hooks.note: Claude PreToolUse Bash gate for Trajectory agent-c worktrees: blocks `gh issue comment` / comment-POST `gh api` calls targeting trajectory-labs-pbc/agent-c when the target issue has a comment newer than the agent's acknowledgment (~/.cache/cr-freshness/agent-c-issue-<N>); the denial message quotes the newest comment so reading it is part of the retry loop. Registered per-worktree in .claude/settings.local.json by sync-agent-c-worktree.sh (never in the tracked agent-c settings.json, which is public team source). Fail-open on API errors. No Codex counterpart: CR issue-posting flows are Claude-only per Trajectory convention (the Codex CR skill copies gate posting back to Claude).

mcp_servers.name: epoch-asana

identity_checks.label: Claude Asana token resolves to pablo@epoch.ai
Codex Asana token resolves to pablo@epoch.ai

mcp_servers.note: Epoch's Asana MCP is project-local and must expose the same server to Claude Code and Codex with the Epoch Asana identity. The audit checks account identity via Asana /users/me and never compares or prints token values.

registrations.note: Tracked global Codex instruction counterpart. The live ~/.codex/AGENTS.md symlink points here because Codex does not apply project_doc_fallback_filenames to $CODEX_HOME.

Mutable live global settings; sync hooks added in place. The Stop hook chain forwards session-end state to Emacs via the fire-and-forget notifier. Session learning capture is handled by the Emacs agent-before-exit path rather than the Stop hook, because Stop fires at turn end rather than true session exit. The 2026-06 Ahrefs guard added block-unguarded-ahrefs-api.sh globally so raw paid shell calls to api.ahrefs.com must go through the live quota wrapper. The 2026-06 GitHub write guard is delegated by pretooluse-bash.sh for Bash commands and registers block-github-guard-edit.sh for Edit/Write self-protection.

Live global Codex hook file is a symlink to the tracked repo file. The tracked file intentionally lives outside a repo-root .codex/ directory so launching Codex from the dotfiles root does not load the same hooks a second time as project-local hooks. Codex-only codex-hook-wrapper entries point at the Emacs package source tree because elpaca/builds contains compiled Elisp but not the wrapper script, and include explicit emacsclient/socket arguments so dispatch targets the active Emacs server. The apply_patch and Edit|Write matchers are collapsed into a single apply_patch|Edit|Write block per event to keep the trust-review list compact under the Codex 0.129 hook trust gate. Codex UserPromptSubmit uses the empty matcher because that event has no tool name. Codex Stop routes through codex-hook-wrapper so Emacs observes turn end; session learning capture is handled by the Emacs agent-before-exit path. PreCompact and PostCompact route through codex-hook-wrapper so the Emacs side observes the full lifecycle. The 2026-05 symlink-drift repair refreshed all eight lifecycle wrapper paths to the active 8.3.0-dev Emacs profile after the standalone codex package learned to replace stale profile-specific wrapper entries instead of appending duplicates. The 2026-06 Ahrefs guard registers block-unguarded-ahrefs-api.sh for shell tools so raw paid api.ahrefs.com commands are denied unless routed through ahrefs-api-guard. The 2026-06 GitHub write guard registers block-github-write-command.sh for shell tools and block-github-guard-edit.sh for allowlist/guard self-protection.

Shared deny-by-default allowlist for agent-initiated GitHub writes. Codex guards block git push and mutating gh operations unless the target repo appears here. The Claude shell guard also permits repos administered by the authenticated gh user, checked live through GitHub permissions and failing closed to this allowlist. The file is empty by default and should include only repos Pablo personally created.

Live global Codex config is a symlink to the tracked repo file; trusted project entries, account-home hook trust entries, plugin enablement state, theme state, desktop/app preferences, desktop turn-end notifications, notice preferences, marketplace timestamps, and App MCP server state are Codex-local and do not have a Claude counterpart, including the nested dotfiles/emacs project allowlist entry used when opening Emacs config work independently, active Elpaca source clones trusted for package work, active repo roots trusted for repeated sessions, and account-specific CODEX_HOME paths such as ~/.codex-epoch. It does not register project-doc fallbacks. bin/skill-prune may add or remove managed [[skills.config]] entries here to disable or re-enable stale paired global and project-local skills; the Claude counterpart is the live untracked ~/.claude/settings.local.json skillOverrides block, with tracked state and absolute project-local paths recorded in skills-disabled.json. The 2026-05 local config refresh trusts the active 8.3.0-dev Emacs profile and modeling-impact work repo, enables the curated Superpowers and Chrome plugins, and re-enables find-skills by removing it from Codex skills.config, skills-disabled.json, and live Claude skillOverrides. The stale local systematic-debugging disable entry was removed after that workflow was delegated to the Superpowers plugin. The former global triage-issues disable entry was removed when that workflow moved to the tlon project-local skill roots. The 2026-06 config audit removed the stale skill-discovery disabled-skill entry after the project-local skill file was removed; this has no tracked Claude counterpart beyond the live untracked skillOverrides state. The 2026-06 Codex config refresh trusts the temporary codex-gt root plus the launchd and macrostrategy repos, and records refreshed ~/.codex-epoch hook trust hashes generated by Codex after the lifecycle wrapper entries changed. The 2026-06 default reasoning effort change sets Codex's model_reasoning_effort to xhigh; this is a Codex-only model preference with no Claude counterpart. Codex 0.129 renamed [features].codex_hooks to [features].hooks; the audit check in bin/ai-config-sync was updated accordingly. The 2026-05 symlink-drift repair promoted live Codex-generated state into this tracked file before restoring ~/.codex/config.toml as a symlink.

Ensures the live ~/.codex/skills directory is a symlink to the tracked codex/skills tree because Codex discovers global skills from ~/.codex/skills/*/SKILL.md. Also installs local gitignored private-skill shims from the encrypted private manifest.

Creates local gitignored private-skill shims under the live skill roots from ai-config-sync.private.json, so explicit private $skill invocations resolve without tracking private names or bodies in the public repo.

Codex-only command allowlist rules, including allowed ~/.codex/AGENTS.md symlink targets and account-specific CODEX_HOME launch commands. There is no Claude counterpart; material policy changes should be recorded here.
